meta.description: Complete TOEFL ITP Listening Comprehension test with Part A (short conversations), Part B (extended conversations), and Part C (talks and lectures)

items.title: Short conversation about library hours
items.ttsText: M: I heard the library extended its hours. W: Yes, now it's open until midnight on weekdays.

questions.question: What does the woman mean?
questions.explanation: The woman states the library is now open until midnight on weekdays.

items.title: Short conversation about bookstore closing
items.ttsText: W: The bookstore is closing in 10 minutes. M: Oh no, I need to buy my textbooks!

questions.question: What will the man probably do?
questions.explanation: The man expresses urgency about buying textbooks before the store closes.

items.title: Short conversation about course selection
items.ttsText: W: I can't decide on an elective course. M: Why not take something related to your major?

questions.question: What does the man suggest?
choices: Choose a course in her field
questions.explanation: The man suggests taking a course related to her major.

items.title: Short conversation about concert tickets
items.ttsText: M: The concert tickets are $75. W: That's way beyond my budget.

questions.question: What does the woman imply?
questions.explanation: The woman indicates the price exceeds her budget.

items.title: Short conversation about scholarship application
items.ttsText: M: Did you submit your scholarship application? W: Not yet, deadline is next Friday.

questions.question: What are they discussing?
questions.explanation: They are discussing a scholarship application, which is financial aid.

items.title: Short conversation about exam performance
items.ttsText: W: How was your chemistry exam? M: I should have studied more.

questions.question: What does the man mean?
questions.explanation: The man expresses regret about not studying more, implying dissatisfaction.

items.title: Short conversation about career fair
items.ttsText: M: Are you going to the career fair? W: Definitely, I need to network.

questions.question: What will the woman do?
questions.explanation: The woman confirms she will attend the career fair.

items.title: Short conversation about statistics help
items.ttsText: M: I'm having trouble with statistics. W: Have you tried the tutoring center?

questions.question: What does the woman suggest?
questions.explanation: The woman suggests trying the tutoring center for help.

items.title: Short conversation about lost essay
items.ttsText: M: My laptop crashed and I lost my essay. W: Didn't you back it up?

questions.question: What is the man's problem?
questions.explanation: The man explains he lost his essay when his laptop crashed.

items.title: Short conversation about study group
items.ttsText: M: Want to join the study group tonight? W: I would, but I have to work.

questions.question: What does the woman mean?
questions.explanation: The woman explains she can't join because she has to work.

items.title: Short conversation about reading assignment
items.ttsText: M: The professor assigned three books. W: I know, that's a lot of reading for one week.

questions.question: What is the topic?
questions.explanation: They are discussing the heavy reading assignment.

items.title: Short conversation about forming study group
items.ttsText: W: Should we form a study group for the midterm? M: Good idea, let's meet at the library.

questions.question: What will they do?
questions.explanation: They agree to form a study group and meet at the library.

items.title: Short conversation about course load
items.ttsText: W: I'm thinking of taking five courses next semester. M: That sounds overwhelming.

questions.question: What does the man imply?
questions.explanation: The man's comment suggests he thinks five courses is too much.

items.title: Short conversation about presentation anxiety
items.ttsText: W: I'm worried about my presentation tomorrow. M: You'll do fine, you prepared well.

questions.question: What is the woman's concern?
questions.explanation: The woman expresses worry about her presentation, indicating nervousness.

items.title: Short conversation about study location
items.ttsText: W: I can't focus in the dorm. M: Try studying at the quiet floor of the library.

questions.question: What does the man suggest?
questions.explanation: The man suggests using the quiet floor of the library.

items.title: Short conversation about parking
items.ttsText: M: All the parking spots are full. W: There's a lot two blocks away.

questions.question: What is the problem?
questions.explanation: The man states all parking spots are full nearby.

items.title: Short conversation about group project progress
items.ttsText: M: Are you done with the group project? W: We're making progress, but still have work to do.

questions.question: What does the woman mean?
questions.explanation: The woman indicates they're progressing but not finished.

items.title: Short conversation about meeting reminder
items.ttsText: W: Don't forget about the department meeting. M: Thanks for reminding me, I almost forgot.

questions.question: What will the man do?
questions.explanation: The man thanks her for the reminder, implying he'll attend.

items.title: Short conversation about campus renovation
items.ttsText: M: I heard they're renovating the student center. W: Yes, it should be done by fall semester.

questions.question: What are they talking about?
questions.explanation: They are discussing renovation of the student center.

items.title: Short conversation about cafeteria prices
items.ttsText: M: Let's eat at the new cafeteria. W: I heard the prices are pretty high.

questions.question: What does the woman imply?
questions.explanation: The woman indicates the cafeteria has high prices.

items.title: Short conversation about lecture opinion
items.ttsText: W: What did you think of the lecture? M: It was informative but too long.

questions.question: What is the man's opinion?
questions.explanation: The man found it informative but thought it was too long.

items.title: Short conversation about writing improvement
items.ttsText: M: I need to improve my writing. W: You should visit the writing center.

questions.question: What does the woman suggest?
questions.explanation: The woman suggests visiting the writing center for help.

items.title: Short conversation about field trip
items.ttsText: M: Did you sign up for the field trip? W: Yes, I'm excited to visit the museum.

questions.question: What is the topic?
questions.explanation: They are discussing a field trip to the museum.

items.title: Short conversation about lab supplies
items.ttsText: W: We need supplies for the experiment. M: I'll go to the lab supply room.

questions.question: What will they do?
questions.explanation: The man will get supplies from the lab supply room.

items.title: Short conversation about joining club
items.ttsText: W: Are you joining the club? M: I'm interested, but I don't have time this semester.

questions.question: What does the man mean?
questions.explanation: The man indicates he doesn't have time this semester.

items.title: Short conversation about account login issue
items.ttsText: M: I can't log into my student account. W: You should contact IT support.

questions.question: What is the problem?
questions.explanation: The man can't log into his student account.

items.title: Short conversation about game invitation
items.ttsText: M: Want to go to the game tonight? W: I have too much homework.

questions.question: What does the woman mean?
questions.explanation: The woman indicates she needs to do homework instead.

items.title: Short conversation about exam date change
items.ttsText: W: The professor changed the exam date. M: That's good, I need more time to prepare.

questions.question: What are they discussing?
questions.explanation: They are discussing a change in the exam date.

items.title: Short conversation about review session
items.ttsText: W: Are you going to the review session? M: Absolutely, I don't want to miss it.

questions.question: What will the man do?
questions.explanation: The man confirms he will attend the review session.

items.title: Short conversation about research paper progress
items.ttsText: M: How's your research paper going? W: I'm still gathering sources.

questions.question: What does the woman imply?
questions.explanation: The woman indicates she's still in the research phase gathering sources.

items.title: Extended conversation about campus housing
items.ttsText: Extended conversation about campus housing options and dormitory rules. Discussion about housing requirements, deposit requirements, advisor recommendations, housing availability, and student decisions on housing applications.

questions.question: What is the main topic?
questions.explanation: The conversation focuses on campus housing options and rules.

questions.question: According to the conversation, what is required?
questions.explanation: A housing deposit is required as discussed in the conversation.

questions.question: What does the speaker suggest?
questions.explanation: The advisor recommends applying early for housing.

questions.question: What can be inferred?
questions.explanation: The discussion implies housing availability is limited.

questions.question: What will the student probably do?
questions.explanation: The student will likely submit the housing application.

items.title: Extended conversation about engineering internships
items.ttsText: Extended conversation about internship opportunities in engineering. Career advisor discusses internship requirements including grades, experience, and recommendations. Discussion about application timeline, deadline at end of month, advisor recommendations to apply to many places, and student plans to update resume.

questions.question: What is the main topic?
questions.explanation: The conversation is about internship opportunities in engineering.

questions.question: According to the speaker, what is important?
questions.explanation: The advisor mentions grades, experience, and recommendations are all important.

questions.question: When is the deadline?
questions.explanation: The deadline is mentioned as end of the month.

questions.question: What does the advisor recommend?
questions.explanation: The advisor recommends applying to many places.

questions.question: What will the student do next?
questions.explanation: The student plans to update their resume.

items.title: Lecture on renewable energy and sustainability
items.ttsText: Professor discusses renewable energy sources and sustainability. Lecture covers solar energy benefits including unlimited supply, provides examples of wind farms, solar panels, and hydroelectric dams. Discussion of future energy trends showing renewables will increase, and professor emphasizes individual responsibility in energy use.

questions.question: What is the main topic of the lecture?
questions.explanation: The lecture focuses on renewable energy sources and sustainability.

questions.question: According to the professor, what is an advantage?
questions.explanation: The professor mentions solar energy has an unlimited supply.

questions.question: What example does the professor give?
questions.explanation: The professor provides examples of all three renewable energy types.

questions.question: What can be inferred?
questions.explanation: The discussion of future trends indicates renewables will increase.

questions.question: What does the professor emphasize?
questions.explanation: The professor emphasizes individual responsibility in energy use.

items.title: Lecture on cognitive psychology and memory
items.ttsText: Lecture on cognitive psychology and memory processes. Professor discusses factors affecting memory including sleep, stress, and nutrition. Memory techniques mentioned include chunking, repetition, and mnemonics. Professor recommends regular practice for students and concludes that memory can be improved with proper techniques.

questions.question: What is the main topic?
questions.explanation: The lecture is about cognitive psychology and memory processes.

questions.question: According to the lecture, what affects memory?
questions.explanation: The professor mentions sleep, stress, and nutrition all affect memory.

questions.question: What method is mentioned?
questions.explanation: The professor describes all three memory techniques.

questions.question: What does the professor suggest?
questions.explanation: The professor recommends regular practice for students.

questions.question: What can be concluded?
questions.explanation: The lecture concludes that memory can be improved with proper techniques.
